items.title: Gradient descent
items.description: Training updates θ by stepping along negative gradients of the loss. Gradient descent is the core optimization loop inside the training phase defined here.

items.title: Stochastic gradient descent (SGD)
items.description: SGD and mini-batch variants scale training to large datasets by estimating gradients on subsets. Still training-only — inference never runs SGD steps.

items.title: Train / validation / test splits
items.description: Training uses the train split with labels; validation/test splits provide held-out (x, y) for evaluation with frozen θ before inference deployment.

items.title: Machine learning
items.description: Every ML system separates learning (training) from application (inference). This topic defines that operational backbone within the broader ML field.

items.title: Supervised learning
items.description: Supervised workflows train on labeled (x, y) then infer ŷ for new x. The paradigm supplies labeled data; this topic defines how that data is used in train vs predict phases.

items.title: Loss functions
items.description: Training minimizes L(ŷ, y) each step; inference does not compute loss because y is unknown. Loss choice shapes what training learns, not how inference runs.
